arts.sum: For the first time under Enzo Maresca, Chelsea have now lost three straight matches in all competitions, after the 2-1 defeat by Aston Villa on Saturday evening. The Blues have fallen out of the Euro...

arts.ttl: Cole Palmer sends clear message during training as Chelsea performances questioned
arts.sum: Chelsea talisman Cole Palmer has struggled for goals and assists in recent weeks, but Enzo Maresca has revealed that the England star has still been bossing it in training

arts.ttl: Chelsea vs Southampton predicted to see if the Blues can avoid four-game losing streak
arts.sum: Chelsea will be desperate for three points when they take on Southampton later today, with the Blues looking to break into the Premier League top four. We predict that Chelsea could set up in a 4-2-3-1 formation, with their line-up as follows: Jorgensen; Acheampong, Tosin, Colwill, Gusto; Caicedo, Cucurella; Palmer, Fernandez, George; Neto.

arts.ttl: Brighton and Southampton ‘sent emissaries’ – PL duo join Manchester United on Monday scouting mission
arts.sum: Brighton, Southampton and Manchester United all had their scouts in attendance at the Estádio do Dragão to watch FC Porto’s 1-1 draw against Vitória de Guimarães on Monday. That’s according to Tuesday’s edition of O Jogo, who state the league fixture ‘attracted a host’ of foreign clubs including Brighton, Southampton and Manchester United. arts.ttl: 2 sources claim player’s agent to have meeting with Chelsea next week – Blues need close to €30m
arts.sum: Chelsea are in the picture to sign River Plate’s Ian Subiabre, according to multiple claims from South America. The 18-year-old made four appearances for River Plate’s first team, while his displays for Argentina at the 2025 South American U20 Championship didn’t go unnoticed as he scored three goals from six games. arts.ttl: Enzo Maresca's net worth, loyal wife, Chelsea manager struggles
arts.sum: Following stints across various clubs in Europe including Sevilla, where he won two UEFA Cups and a Copa del Rey, Maresca ended his 19-year playing career retiring at Hellas Verona in 2017. Following Chelsea’s bad run since the start of the year, many pundits have speculated Maresca might be running out of time.

arts.ttl: Chelsea protest truth revealed as Roman Abramovich demand and brutal Todd Boehly reality clear
arts.sum: "I know I wanted Chelsea fans to unite in making our feelings clear to [Co-owner, Behdad] Eghbali that this squad, this squad building approach, their erosion of everything that proceeded them, including the academy, their fixation on playing'style' and their constant tampering with the club's brand and matchday experience is not acceptable for us." "The sporting directors, ticket pricing, recruitment model, treatment of academy players, and long-serving staff, the mess around stadium plans and the CPO are all concerns," says Chelsea fan and content creator Daniel Childs to football.london .

arts.ttl: Alan Shearer describes Chelsea as 'weird' for still lacking three major things in their squad
arts.sum: Alan Shearer has taken a swipe at Chelsea by claiming their squad is still lacking in a few key areas despite the investment in recent...

arts.ttl: ‘Not happy’: John Obi Mikel says Didier Drogba really didn’t like one manager at Chelsea and wanted him sacked
arts.sum: Didier Drogba delivered some of Chelsea’s greatest moments under a number of different managers. Didier Drogba was key to Chelsea’s early success under Jose Mourinho,...

arts.ttl: Arsenal 'favourites' over Chelsea for €85m WONDERKID transfer
arts.sum: Gittens has reportedly been told by Dortmund that he will be allowed to leave this summer despite having three years remaining on his contract, with the German side needing to raise funds if they do not qualify for the Champions League. Arsenal have been among the sides who have closely watched Gittens in recent weeks and, with Dortmund likely to miss out on Champions League football next season, they could be willing to part ways with their young star for around €85 million.
